other_observers: Tammy Sanders, Mark Chavez, Glen Walbek, John Drummond, Dick Pricket, Steve Stehoviac, Joey Kellner, Doug Faulkner, Mark Obmascik, Larry Modesit, Joe Roller, Bill Schmoker and many more.; Original Observer is Mike Henwood. Others observing this bird at a different time were: Chris Wood, Allison Kondler, Steve Mlodinow, Will Burt, Paul Hurtado.
duration: Several seconds to 5 minutes at a time.
specific_location: Bear Creek Lake Park along the north road near the boat launch at the Coyote Crossing Trail sign and gate. It was feeding on the wooden foot bridge at that location.
description: Discovered on 11/19/12 by Mike Henwood. Many photos taken. Gray face and crown extending down the back and side of the head with a black stripe either side of the gray on the back of the head and nape. White eye-ring. Black forehead and malar stripe. Light lores. Bill mostly yellowish. White rump and lower back when flying. Black wings with small white wingbar and large buffy orange wingbar and same color shoulder. The buffy orange extends across the breast, on the throat and chin and on the sides. White belly and undertail coverts. The back was light brown with black markings. Tail mostly black. About House Finch size.Behaviors: Feeding on the ground with Tree Sparrow, House Finches. Would first land in a tree above the feeding area at Bear Creek Lake Park and then fly down to feed with the other ground feeding birds.Call: None heardPlumage: Immature or Basic
